title: Optical Diffraction Tomography (ODT) — 3D refractive-index imaging
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (genesis fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical ODT literature (Wolf 1969 diffraction tomography theorem; Kamilov et al. learned reconstruction).

E.description: ODT illuminates a weakly scattering 3D object with coherent plane waves at multiple angles; for each angle the complex scattered field is measured holographically. Under first Born / Rytov approximations, each scattered field is a 2D slice of the 3D Fourier transform of the refractive-index contrast on a cap of the Ewald sphere. Rotating the illumination fills the 3D k-space volume (up to the missing cone).
E.forward_model: U_s(k_x, k_y; theta) = i * k / (2 * sqrt(k^2 - |k_perp|^2)) * F{n(r) - n_0}(k_perp, k_z(theta)) + n_noise
E.world_state_x: 3D refractive-index perturbation (n(r) - n_0) on a H x W x D grid; complex (absorption + phase)
E.observation_y: K complex scattered fields U_s(x, y; theta_k), k = 1..K (obtained via off-axis digital holography per angle)
physical_parameters_theta: K illumination angles {theta_k} (spherical cap in k-space)
pixel size dx, depth step dz

W.regime: Ill-posed along the missing-cone axis (cone of k-space unreachable by finite-NA illumination). Regularized inversion (TV, deep priors) fills the cone with smoothness assumptions. Non-linear Beyond-Born solvers (LS-LT, SEAGLE) handle multiple scattering but become strongly non-convex.

C.solver_class: FBP-ODT (filtered backprojection over Ewald cap), Rytov inversion, TV-regularized ADMM, Multi-slice (beam-propagation), DeepODT / UNet-ODT
C.error_bound: ||n_hat - n||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(K) + C2 * delta_missing_cone; missing-cone artifacts dominate at NA < 0.8
C.complexity: O(K * H * W * log(H*W)) for linear Born-Rytov; O(K * H * W * D) per iteration for multi-slice non-linear
